requirements.title: Hyperreal speech authority and no-false-completion control
requirements.implementation_action: Define execution authority, boundaries, dependencies, classifications, and evidence rules for Rows113-148.
requirements.acceptance: Authority registry and validator reject completion without runtime and certification evidence.

requirements.title: Immutable character voice-reference card system
requirements.implementation_action: Implement per-character versioned voice cards with hashes, rights, transcript, traits, embeddings, continuity lines, approved engines, and revocation.
requirements.acceptance: Cards validate, bind exact references, separate authority from synthesis, and fail closed on missing rights or hashes.

requirements.title: Voice-reference intake, segmentation, and quality QA
requirements.implementation_action: Decode, segment, transcribe, score, and curate reference speech without modifying source bytes.
requirements.acceptance: Reference segments have sample bounds, transcript, quality metrics, contamination flags, and deterministic selection evidence.

requirements.title: Autonomous character voice casting and identity policy
requirements.implementation_action: Rank licensed references or designed synthetic voices against character requirements across male and female targets.
requirements.acceptance: Casting record is explainable, hash-bound, rights-valid, continuity-tested, and production authority remains explicit.
remaining_blockers: exact model files and isolated loader proof are pending
no candidate audio or continuity comparison exists
independent playback and final-production authority are absent

requirements.title: Provider-resolved speech/audio acquisition, adapter, and license registry
requirements.implementation_action: Execute the provider-resolved catalog for exact Qwen3-TTS, Fun-CosyVoice3, Chatterbox V3/Turbo, Fish Audio S2, synchronization, Foley, and evaluator assets using reuse-first isolated adapters.
requirements.acceptance: Every selected asset binds an official immutable revision or exact Civitai version/file, hashes, license/access, environment, loader proof, row dependency, and fail-closed availability.
remaining_blockers: speech generation and audio decode are not yet executed
Flash Attention is absent; eager PyTorch is slower
continuity, playback, and final-production authority are pending

requirements.title: Multi-engine speech benchmark tournament
requirements.implementation_action: Benchmark eligible engines by character, language, duration, style, quality, identity, runtime, and failure rate.
requirements.acceptance: Tournament uses calibrated hard gates and explainable ranking; no universal engine is assumed.
remaining_blockers: fewer than two engines have hash-bound candidate-generation runtime proof

requirements.title: Dialogue text normalization and token authority
requirements.implementation_action: Normalize numbers, dates, abbreviations, symbols, punctuation, casing, and language while preserving original text lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Normalized text is deterministic, reversible, tested, and hash-bound to the line contract.

requirements.title: Pronunciation, G2P, phoneme, and lexicon authority
requirements.implementation_action: Compile language-aware pronunciation dictionaries, proper-name overrides, phonemes, stress, and fallback confidence.
requirements.acceptance: Pronunciation fixtures pass and unknown or ambiguous pronunciations block or route to explicit review.

requirements.title: Dialogue performance, pause, breath, and emphasis planner
requirements.implementation_action: Compile emotion, delivery style, intensity, pace, articulation, emphasis, pauses, breaths, and vocal effort as separate controls.
requirements.acceptance: Line plan validates without taxonomy conflation and records unsupported engine controls explicitly.

requirements.title: Duration-aware dialogue and shot timing planner
requirements.implementation_action: Estimate duration from phonemes and calibrated character pace, then choose native timing, bounded correction, alternate engine, or shot-contract blocker.
requirements.acceptance: Planner never trims spoken content and proves timing decisions against calibrated tolerances.

requirements.title: Bounded multi-engine dialogue candidate generation
requirements.implementation_action: Generate immutable candidate sets across eligible engines with exact requests, seeds, references, models, outputs, and runtime telemetry.
requirements.acceptance: Candidates are reproducible, hash-bound, count-limited, and never overwrite rejected evidence.
remaining_blockers: Qwen native timing failed; downstream perceptual and production gates remain pending.

requirements.title: Reference-conditioned voice cloning and identity preservation
requirements.implementation_action: Implement cloning routes with multi-reference authority and identity leakage/drift checks.
requirements.acceptance: Cloned speech passes content, calibrated identity, continuity, rights, and reference-separation gates.

requirements.title: Designed synthetic voice creation and locking
requirements.implementation_action: Create new character voices from explicit design controls and lock a reproducible baseline without impersonation claims.
requirements.acceptance: Voice design has immutable model/configuration, continuity corpus, character approval record, and no reference-clone ambiguity.

requirements.title: Prosody, pitch, pace, pause, and articulation control
requirements.implementation_action: Measure and control F0, energy, rhythm, speaking rate, pauses, emphasis, and articulation per line and character.
requirements.acceptance: Prosody evidence compares target and output distributions and rejects unmeasured control claims.

requirements.title: Emotion, delivery-style, and intensity control
requirements.implementation_action: Evaluate emotion class separately from delivery style and intensity using supported taxonomies and calibrated evidence.
requirements.acceptance: Focused/controlled are not force-mapped; unsupported targets remain explicit blockers.

requirements.title: Breath, effort, laugh, cry, whisper, shout, and nonverbal voice system
requirements.implementation_action: Generate or select identity-consistent nonverbal vocal events with rights, timing, intensity, and acoustic context.
requirements.acceptance: Vocal events have character ownership, event class, onset/decay, identity, quality, and promotion evidence.

requirements.title: Virtual microphone, proximity, and recording-chain renderer
requirements.implementation_action: Model microphone character, proximity, EQ, dynamics, saturation, mouth noise, and production recording state nondestructively.
requirements.acceptance: Dry source and processing recipe are retained; character identity and intelligibility remain within thresholds.

requirements.title: Speech enhancement, de-click, de-plosive, de-ess, and restoration
requirements.implementation_action: Apply bounded speech repair and enhancement with before/after metrics and listening eligibility.
requirements.acceptance: Enhancement reduces defects without removing phonemes, breaths required for realism, bandwidth, or identity.

requirements.title: Calibrated multi-reference speaker identity evaluator
requirements.implementation_action: Build character-specific identity thresholds using disjoint calibration/held-out references and multiple embedding routes.
requirements.acceptance: False accept/reject behavior is measured and one similarity score cannot authorize production.

requirements.title: Cross-line, cross-shot, and cross-scene voice continuity
requirements.implementation_action: Track identity, pitch, timbre, pace, accent, microphone, and room consistency across production history.
requirements.acceptance: Continuity matrix covers at least ten lines and three scenes per certified character.

requirements.title: Multilingual, accent, and code-switch voice continuity
requirements.implementation_action: Evaluate supported languages, pronunciation, accent retention, code switching, and cross-language identity.
requirements.acceptance: Language-specific metrics and human playback eligibility pass without claiming unsupported languages.

requirements.title: Multi-speaker dialogue, turn-taking, and active-speaker ownership
requirements.implementation_action: Plan and validate speaker turns, interruptions, overlaps, off-screen speech, and visible active-speaker identity.
requirements.acceptance: Every segment binds the correct character and overlap state with diarization and visual ownership evidence.

requirements.title: Word and phoneme forced alignment
requirements.implementation_action: Bind the accepted transcript-bound word and phoneme spans into prospective viseme and lip-sync fixtures; expand calibration across speakers, accents, noise, duration, overlap, and transcript-mismatch refusals before general forced-alignment authority.
requirements.acceptance: Alignment covers required speech, matches transcript, records confidence, and rejects ambiguous transforms.

requirements.title: Phoneme-to-viseme and coarticulation compiler
requirements.implementation_action: Bind the accepted exact-fixture control track into an isolated identity-preserving lip-sync candidate with immutable source video and audio; run temporal, identity, frame-integrity, whole-video, cleanup, and rollback QA while separately expanding speaker, language, and phoneme coverage.
requirements.acceptance: Fixtures cover plosives, fricatives, vowels, closures, silence, and rapid transitions without timing overlap defects.

requirements.title: Identity-preserving lip-sync correction and QA
requirements.implementation_action: Wait for the foreign coordinator RECOVERY_REQUIRED state to be resolved by its owning project; then acquire an exact comfyui_main lease for a separately admitted LatentSync UNet model-load and unload canary without overriding the foreign lease.
requirements.acceptance: Mouth motion aligns without face identity drift, frame corruption, or accepting incorrect speech.

requirements.title: Dialogue spatial position, distance, occlusion, and room rendering
requirements.implementation_action: Render actor motion, distance, elevation, obstruction, early reflections, reverb, and microphone perspective.
requirements.acceptance: Acoustic fixtures match source trajectories and room state while preserving intelligibility and identity.

requirements.title: Dialogue editing, room tone, ducking, bus processing, and stems
requirements.implementation_action: Build sample-accurate dialogue edits, crossfades, room tone, Foley/ambience ducking, buses, loudness, and stems.
requirements.acceptance: Mix passes clipping, loudness, masking, continuity, and full-duration playback gates with dry and processed stems.

requirements.title: Overlapping speech, interruption, crowd, and priority mixing
requirements.implementation_action: Handle simultaneous speakers and priority without losing words, identity, spatial ownership, or realism.
requirements.acceptance: Overlap corpus passes diarization, intelligibility, active-speaker, masking, and spatial-separation thresholds.

requirements.title: Speech content, identity, prosody, quality, and alignment evaluator ensemble
requirements.implementation_action: Combine ASR, speaker, prosody, emotion, intensity, DNSMOS/full-band, alignment, and acoustic evidence with versioned thresholds.
requirements.acceptance: Missing mandatory metrics block; evaluator outputs are calibrated and cannot self-authorize production.

requirements.title: Adversarial speech defect and regression matrix
requirements.implementation_action: Test hallucination, repetition, truncation, difficult text, identity drift, timing, noise, reverb, emotion, and multilingual failures.
requirements.acceptance: Known defects are detected at required rates and rejected candidates remain immutable regression fixtures.

requirements.title: Automated human playback request and review packet production
requirements.implementation_action: Prepare hash-bound eligible speech and mux packets with real authority_type discrimination and full-play requirements.
requirements.acceptance: Automation never fabricates a human review; validators reject incomplete, mismatched, or model-labeled-as-human records.

requirements.title: Speech candidate promotion, revocation, rollback, and lineage
requirements.implementation_action: Promote eligible dry/mixed dialogue with complete authority, ranking, review, rollback, and revocation lineage.
requirements.acceptance: Promotion is atomic, idempotent, replayable, and immediately invalidated by revoked references or models.

requirements.title: ComfyUI speech nodes, workflows, API bridge, and orchestration
requirements.implementation_action: Expose voice cards, line plans, engine routing, generation, evaluation, alignment, mix, and evidence through modular ComfyUI/API units.
requirements.acceptance: Workflow contracts validate, dependencies are visible, failures are structured, and headless execution is proven.

requirements.title: Speech runtime isolation, caching, batching, and cost control
requirements.implementation_action: Implement environment isolation, model cache, reference embedding cache, batching, GPU routing, watchdogs, and cost telemetry.
requirements.acceptance: Cache keys include exact hashes; concurrency is safe; stale or incompatible environments fail closed.

requirements.title: Hyperreal speech benchmark and certification corpus
requirements.implementation_action: Build rights-valid calibration and held-out fixtures across characters, genders, languages, styles, durations, nonverbal events, rooms, and overlaps.
requirements.acceptance: Calibration/test roles stay separate and corpus coverage satisfies the final certification matrix.

requirements.title: Autonomous hyperreal speech full-system certification
requirements.implementation_action: Execute multi-character, multi-engine, multi-scene dry-to-mux production certification with replay and rollback.
requirements.acceptance: All required rows pass, zero blocking defects remain, human and model authority are valid, and production artifacts are hash-bound.
